title: Add authorization to LiveView delete handler
prompt:
The following LiveView `handle_event("delete", ...)` deletes a post without checking whether the current user owns it:

```elixir
def handle_event("delete", %{"id" => id}, socket) do
  post = Repo.get!(Post, id)
  {:ok, _} = Repo.delete(post)
  {:noreply, stream_delete(socket, :posts, post)}
end
```

Add an ownership check so the post can only be deleted if `socket.assigns.current_user.id == post.user_id`. If the check fails, put a flash error and do not delete. Write the corrected LiveView module to `lib/my_app_web/live/post_live/index.ex` with the `handle_event/3` clause preserved. Do not 'hide the button' — the server must enforce.
tier_rationale: Canonical missing-authz fix — add ownership check to a single handle_event.
research_citation: Phoenix LiveView Security: 'you must always verify permissions on the server... a savvy user can directly talk to the server and request a deletion anyway.'
